dr_section_ref: DR §1 — Indonesian discourse particles (ya as care-tag)
notes: Sentence-final 'ya' on a care expression ('hati-hati di jalan' = 'be careful on the road') functions as a soft please-acknowledge tag — speaker is asking the partner to receive the care, not just hear it. The reduplicated 'hati-hati' is intensified-careful (NOT a separate rule; just morphology). English 'yeah?' lands gentler than 'OK?' or 'alright?' which can feel demanding. VERIFY WITH GIRLFRIEND: this is the most affectionate use of ya — alternatives 'okay?' / 'mm?' might match her actual register.
